name: formatto
description: A flexible Ruby formatter for VS Code, supporting rubyfmt, rufo and standardrb.
author: damolinx

formatto.additionalSupportedExtensions.markdownDescription: Additional file extensions to accept for formatting, in addition to the [formatter](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%5B%22formatto.formatter%22%5D)'s built-in supported extensions. These extensions only apply to files whose language is already recognized as `ruby`, `gemfile`, or `erb`. Your selected formatter must still support the file syntax; otherwise formatting will fail (see logs).

formatto.enableRangeFormatting.markdownDescription: Enable **experimental** support of the **Format Selection** command (requires restart). `rubyfmt` does not support this natively so formatting may be inaccurate or differ from **Format Document**.

formatto.excludePatterns.markdownDescription: Glob patterns for files that should not be formatte, e.g. `**/__package.rb`

formatto.formatter.description: Select which formatter Formatto should use.

formatto.formatPendingChanges.includeSta.markdownDescription: Include staged changes when running **Format Pending Changes**.

formatto.rubyfmtArgs.markdownDescription: Additional arguments to pass to `rubyfmt`, one argument per entry. Use `rubyfmt --help` to check for available flags.

formatto.rubyfmtPath.markdownDescription:
Path to the `rubyfmt` executable. The following tokens are available:

- `${userHome}`: User home directory.
- `${workspaceFolder}`: Current workspace root directory.

If the value is a bare command (e.g. `rubyfmt`), it will be resolved from the current environment's PATH.
formatto.rubyfmtPath.patternErrorMessage: Value must be empty, or a valid path

formatto.verifyRubyfmt.markdownDescription: Verify that `rubyfmt` is available at the resolved [path](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%5B%22formatto.rubyfmtPath%22%5D).

formatto.rufoArgs.markdownDescription: Additional arguments to pass to `rufo`, one argument per entry. Use `rufo --help` to check for available flags.

formatto.rufoPath.markdownDescription:
Path to the `rufo` executable. The following tokens are available:

- `${userHome}`: User home directory.
- `${workspaceFolder}`: Current workspace root directory.

If the value is a bare command (e.g. `rufo`), it will be resolved from the current environment's PATH.
formatto.rufoPath.patternErrorMessage: Value must be empty, or a valid path

formatto.rufoPreferBundler.markdownDescription: Prefer running `rufo` via `bundle exec` when a Gemfile is present and no explicit `formatto.rufoPath` is set.

formatto.verifyRufo.markdownDescription: Verify that `rufo` is available at the resolved [path](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%5B%22formatto.rufoPath%22%5D).

formatto.standardrbArgs.markdownDescription: Additional arguments to pass to `standardrb`, one argument per entry. Use `standardrb --help` to check for available flags.

markdownEnumDescriptions: Write the current editor contents to a temporary file, format and apply changes back.
Force-save the current editor to disk before formatting.
formatto.standardrbFormattingMode.markdownDescription: `standardrb` can only format files on disk, so Formatto satisfies this requirement using the selected behavior whenever the target editor has pending changes.

formatto.standardrbPath.markdownDescription:
Path to the `standardrb` executable. The following tokens are available:

- `${userHome}`: User home directory.
- `${workspaceFolder}`: Current workspace root directory.

If the value is a bare command (e.g. `standardrb`), it will be resolved from the current environment's PATH.
formatto.standardrbPath.patternErrorMessage: Value must be empty, or a valid path

formatto.verifyStandardrb.markdownDescription: Verify that `standardrbPath` is available at the resolved [path](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%5B%22formatto.standardrbPath%22%5D).

scripts.compile: npm run ts:check-types && npm run ts:compile
